prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr! Listen close, ye scurvy dog, and I’ll tell ye why the sky be blue as a mermaid’s eye!

’Tis the sun’s own light, ye see—white as a ghost’s shroud—but when it sails through the heavens and crashes upon the air itself, the wee bits o’ blue get scattered every which way like cannon shot in a squall! The other colors—red, yellow, green—they be heavier, like bilge in the hold, and keep sailin’ straight on. But blue? Blue be light and spry, bouncin’ off the very breath o’ the sky till the whole dome above us glows with it!

So next time ye squint up at that endless blue, know it’s the sun’s own trick—scatterin’ blue light like doubloons flung from Davy Jones’ locker! Now quit yer gawkin’ and swab the deck, or I’ll feed ye to the kraken!
